mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in >70% of instances following the trigger, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series decouples, or combined Rank IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 2-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, prompting portfolio de-risking that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >65% of periods following the GDP slowdown trigger, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized economic expansion, boosting equity risk appetite and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when labor and output data diverge, or combined predictive accuracy drops below 55% in walk-forward validation.
